interactions.description: Increased bleeding risk — warfarin inhibits clotting factor synthesis, aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation. Combined effect dramatically raises hemorrhage risk.
Coumadin is warfarin. Major bleeding interaction — both inhibit clotting via different mechanisms.
NSAIDs increase anticoagulant effect of warfarin and cause GI bleeding, raising hemorrhage risk.
Serotonin syndrome risk — sertraline (SSRI) and tramadol both increase serotonergic activity. Symptoms: agitation, hyperthermia, seizures, death.
Serotonin syndrome risk — fluoxetine (SSRI) and tramadol both increase serotonergic activity.
Any SSRI combined with tramadol raises serotonin syndrome risk.
Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4, causing simvastatin accumulation and myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk.
Amiodarone increases digoxin plasma levels by ~70%, risking toxicity: bradycardia, heart block, fatal arrhythmia.
ACE inhibitors raise serum potassium. Supplemental potassium can cause dangerous hyperkalemia.
Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, reducing clopidogrel activation and antiplatelet effect.
Ibuprofen (NSAID) reduces renal clearance of methotrexate, causing toxic accumulation.
Ciprofloxacin dramatically increases tizanidine levels via CYP1A2 inhibition — severe hypotension risk.
Severe hypotension — both cause vasodilation via different pathways. Can be fatal.
Contrast dye can cause acute kidney injury, leading to metformin accumulation and lactic acidosis.

abrupt_withdrawal_dangerous.risk: Rebound tachycardia, hypertension, angina, myocardial infarction
